ukg-4x4-pluck-driver.description: Full-song blueprint for a 4-on-floor UKG cut with a dark bass bed, soft pad support, bright pluck hook, and compact transition FX.
ukg-4x4-pluck-driver.energy_profile: Controlled 4-on-floor drive with a front-and-center pluck hook and enough open space for vocal chops later.

harmonic_plan.movement_note: Keep the hook rhythmic and repetitive while the harmonic bed does the width work.
harmonic_plan.bass_strategy: Bass stays root-heavy with short approach notes and leaves room for the pluck to define the motif.

ukg-4x4-pluck-driver.drum_strategy: Program a club-weighted 4x4 kick lane first, then use tops and one loop layer for motion rather than overfilling the percussion grid.
ukg-4x4-pluck-driver.melodic_strategy: The hook pluck owns the memorable phrase; the support pad should never outshine it.
arrangement_notes: Intro starts with drums, atmosphere, and a filtered pad teaser before the pluck fully arrives.
Drop B should add one extra answering percussion or FX element so it feels upgraded, not repeated.
Leave the vocal space lane empty until the groove already works without it.
reserved_spaces: One vocal-chop lane for a later call-and-response phrase
One transition-impact lane at the end of the break
One atmosphere lane for intro and outro air

drum_loop_layer.purpose: Optional brushed garage loop to add movement under the pluck sections.

vocal_space.purpose: Keep a lane open for a later female chop or spoken phrase.

bass-foundation.job_in_track: anchor the drop with a mono-safe low-end line that leaves rhythmic space around the pluck hook
bass-foundation.arrangement_role: present in both drops, lightly teased in the break

support-pad.job_in_track: supply width and emotional wash without stealing focus from the pluck
support-pad.arrangement_role: teased in intro, full in drops, widened in break

hook-pluck.job_in_track: carry the recognisable motif and define the record's top-line identity
hook-pluck.arrangement_role: introduced late in intro, featured in drops, partially muted in break

transition-fx.job_in_track: mark arrangement boundaries and stop the repetitive pluck loop from feeling static
transition-fx.arrangement_role: used at end of intro, break, and final drop pickup

ukg-4x4-lead-driver.description: Full-song blueprint for a 4-on-floor UKG arrangement where a wide chord bed supports a brighter lead and a clean support stab.
ukg-4x4-lead-driver.energy_profile: More melodic than the pluck-driver brief, with the lead acting as the hook and the stab filling the response lane.

harmonic_plan.movement_note: The lead should phrase across the bar line while the support stab answers inside the groove.
harmonic_plan.bass_strategy: Bass stays simple and grounded so the melodic layers can carry the personality.

ukg-4x4-lead-driver.drum_strategy: Use the same 4x4 anchor as the pluck driver but leave more top-end space for the lead and response stab.
ukg-4x4-lead-driver.melodic_strategy: The lead owns the singable phrase; the stab is there to answer and reinforce rhythm, not compete.
arrangement_notes: The lead should not fully appear in the first 8 bars of the intro.
Drop B should add one extra response phrase or wider delay throw to feel larger.
If a vocal space is filled later, it should live between lead phrases rather than under every bar.
reserved_spaces: One vocal lane for a phrase-based topline
One background texture lane that can stay empty if the hook already carries enough air

atmosphere.purpose: Needed to keep the melodic intro from sounding naked before the lead enters.

bass-foundation.job_in_track: keep the low end stable and uncluttered beneath a more melodic top stack
bass-foundation.arrangement_role: present in both drops, filtered down in the break

chord-bed.job_in_track: create width and harmonic glue behind the lead without fogging the center
chord-bed.arrangement_role: teased early, fully open in the break, tucked under the lead in drops

main-lead.job_in_track: carry the core hook and establish the record's emotional identity
main-lead.arrangement_role: appears late in intro, dominates both drops, strips back in break

support-stab.job_in_track: answer the lead rhythmically and add percussive harmony
support-stab.arrangement_role: mostly drop-only, with one teaser phrase before Drop A

ukg-2step-dark-stab.description: Full-song blueprint for a darker 2-step UKG record built around a bass/reese conversation and a chopped stab hook.
ukg-2step-dark-stab.energy_profile: Darker and more syncopated than the 4x4 briefs, with the stab acting like a vocal replacement and the bass stack carrying most of the aggression.

harmonic_plan.movement_note: The dark pad handles the atmosphere while the chopped stab and bass conversation create the hook.
harmonic_plan.bass_strategy: The main bass holds the center while the reese answers in the low-mid stereo field without duplicating the sub.

ukg-2step-dark-stab.drum_strategy: Build the groove around kick, snare/clap, hats, and one rolling top layer, then let empty space do as much work as the hits.
ukg-2step-dark-stab.melodic_strategy: Treat the bright stab like a chopped vocal feature: memorable, clipped, and rhythm-first.
arrangement_notes: Intro should hint at the dark pad and top groove before the full bass conversation lands.
Break should widen the dark pad and vocal-space lane while muting one of the bass roles.
Switch-up should feel like a deliberate left turn, not just a shorter break.
reserved_spaces: One vocal chop lane that can mirror the stab rhythm later
One breakbeat or garage-loop lane for Groove B only
One atmosphere lane for the intro and break

drum_loop_layer.purpose: Groove B should gain a loop layer or break fragment so the second half develops.

perc_fill.purpose: The 2-step pocket needs one answering percussion or rim move.

bass-foundation.job_in_track: own the center low end and drive the groove with short, punchy phrases
bass-foundation.arrangement_role: present in both groove sections, filtered in the break, simplified in the switch-up

secondary-reese.job_in_track: answer the main bass in the low-mid stereo field without doubling the true sub lane
secondary-reese.arrangement_role: drop-heavy, partly muted in the break, widened in Groove B

dark-pad.job_in_track: supply tension and negative-space atmosphere behind the rhythm section
dark-pad.arrangement_role: intro and break feature layer, lower in the drops

chop-stab.job_in_track: function like the vocal-identifying hook even if no actual vocal sample is added
chop-stab.arrangement_role: sparingly introduced before Groove A, hook focus in both grooves, echoed in the switch-up

ukg-140-og-bounce-driver.description: Full-song blueprint for a 140 BPM modern UKG / speed-garage record with Kettama-level pressure, Interplanetary Criminal bounce, Soul Mass Transit System-style rolling bass language, and enough harmonic depth to stand instrumentally before any future sample version.
ukg-140-og-bounce-driver.energy_profile: Hard 4-on-floor pressure with rolling UKG low-end, strong bounce from loops and hats, and a disciplined midrange lane that still lets the instrumental stand on its own.

harmonic_plan.movement_note: Let the harmony feel musically rich like Sammy Virji or Y U QT, but keep the actual hook phrase economical and instrumental-led rather than depending on a later sample.
harmonic_plan.bass_strategy: Bass owns a rolling 1-6-3-7 backbone with internal motion and stronger phrase-end releases, while the answer lane stays in the melodic/harmonic register and never steals the true sub.

ukg-140-og-bounce-driver.drum_strategy: Build from a body-forward 4x4 core, then add swingy tops, filtered loop bounce, and one restrained break layer so the groove feels huge without sacrificing the pocket.
ukg-140-og-bounce-driver.melodic_strategy: The instrumental hook should behave like a response lane rather than a full topline: enough identity to carry the record now through rhythm and timbre, but disciplined enough that a later re-version could trade in a vocal or sample.
arrangement_notes: Intro should establish kick, tuned hats, and filtered loop bounce before revealing the full rolling bass language.
Drop A should feel bass-and-groove led first, with the hook-response lane entering as punctuation rather than constant chatter.
Break should widen the chord bed, thin the drums, and let the instrumental atmosphere own the center before any future sample version exists.
Drop B should add harmonic bloom and one stronger phrase-end answer lane while keeping the low end just as disciplined as Drop A.

arrangement_sections.goal: Establish the 4x4 kick, tuned tops, filtered loop bounce, and atmosphere without exposing the full bass roll.
Tease the rolling bass shape and chord width while still leaving the center lane and hook-response mostly open.
Reveal the full bass spine, restrained hook punctuation, and the first complete bounce pocket.
Develop the first drop with tighter percussion answers, loop energy, and one stronger response figure without overcrowding the center.
Widen the chord bed, thin the drums, and create real headroom for a future sample lane or spoken phrase.
Bring the full groove back with harder loop support, harmonic bloom, and a more confident phrase-end answer lane.
Add the strongest answering phrase or sample response of the track while preserving low-end discipline and kick authority.
Strip back to a DJ-safe mix-out with drums, bass suggestion, and enough atmosphere to exit cleanly.

reserved_spaces: One optional chopped vocal or spoken sample lane in the center midrange for a future re-version
One phrase-end response lane for hook or answer-stab punctuation in Drop B
One transition-impact lane at the end of the break and final drop pickup
One atmosphere lane for intro air and break lift

drum_loop_layer.purpose: Filtered garage or hard-house loop layer to supply bounce and width around the 4x4 core.

vocal_space.purpose: Reserve a true center lane for a future chopped vocal, adlib, or one-shot phrase.

percussion_or_rim.purpose: One answering percussion or rim move to keep the groove bouncing between kick and clap.

atmosphere.purpose: Needed to stop the intro and break from feeling too dry before the full stack lands.

bass-foundation.job_in_track: own the centered sub and rolling low-end punch while keeping the phrasing bouncy and modern UKG rather than overdesigned
bass-foundation.arrangement_role: present in both drops, filtered in the intro, simplified in the break

og-reese-answer.job_in_track: add a warm phrase-end answer in the melodic register without duplicating the true sub lane or becoming a second bassline
og-reese-answer.arrangement_role: drop punctuation only, absent in the break, more decisive in Drop B

chord-bed.job_in_track: carry the musical robustness and width so the track feels like a song rather than just a bass tool
chord-bed.arrangement_role: teased in intro, widened in break, tucked behind the groove in drops

hook-response.job_in_track: provide a memorable instrumental answer that can carry the record on its own before any later vocal or sample version exists
hook-response.arrangement_role: sparing in Drop A, more active in Drop B, mostly absent in the break
